steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must correct the misunderstanding that Pangaea formed before Kenorland. A good answer must explicitly establish which supercontinent came first, using the dates from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Forming and breaking apart over hundreds of millions of years, supercontinents are made up of most or all of Earth"s continents.': Describes the supercontinent cycle generally; never mentions Kenorland or Pangaea"s order. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Kenorland formed about 2.6 billion years ago, long before Pangaea"s formation about 300 million years ago.': Directly corrects the misunderstanding with both dates, making the chronological order unmistakable. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Pangaea and Kenorland formed at different times in Earth"s history.': Notes they differ in timing but never says which came first — leaves the misunderstanding uncorrected. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Kenorland and Pangaea were both supercontinents, single landmasses made up of most or all of the Earth"s continents.': Defines supercontinents; says nothing about when each formed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
